title: Limiter
summary: Hard ceiling that prevents the signal from exceeding 0dBFS. Lives on the Master channel, last in chain. Safety net — not a creative effect.
steps: Add Limiter as the very last device on the Master channel (after all other effects)
Set Ceiling to -0.3dBFS — this is the absolute maximum output level
Set Lookahead to 3ms — allows the limiter to react fractionally before peaks arrive, reducing distortion
Enable Soft Clip — adds gentle saturation at the ceiling rather than hard brick-wall clipping
Watch the Gain Reduction meter — it should catch occasional peaks only (1–2dB reduction on the loudest hits)
If the meter is constantly showing 3dB+ reduction: something upstream is too loud — fix the levels, don't rely on the limiter to fix it
tips: The Limiter's job is safety, not loudness. If you want louder mixes, push the level before the limiter
Soft Clip ON is recommended — without it, the ceiling is a hard clip. Soft Clip curves the peaks gently, similar to analogue tape saturation
Never use Limiter on individual tracks as a creative effect — use Saturator for track-level harmonic distortion
A master limiter catching more than 3dB constantly means the mix is too hot. Lower the main fader or the loudest tracks, then re-examine
practice_hint: Add a Limiter to your Master channel in any guided build session. Set Ceiling -0.3dBFS. Check the gain reduction meter while the track plays — it should be barely moving.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
